tool_args.raw_text: MTA Long Island Rail Road (LIRR – which owns the Branch)

tool_args.label: New York and Atlantic Railway (NY&A)
tool_args.raw_text: the line has subsequently been leased out to the New York and Atlantic (NY&A) Railway, which provides rail freight service to Long Island and to industrial customers along the Branch in Queens.

payload.role: operates freight service on the Lower Montauk Branch

tool_args.label: Council Member Elizabeth Crowley
tool_args.raw_text: sponsored by New York City District 30 Council Member Elizabeth Crowley

tool_args.label: Lower Montauk Branch
tool_args.raw_text: The Branch is a two-track, nine-mile, non-electrified rail line running through the borough of Queens from Jamaica to the east, terminating in Long Island City in the west.

payload.description: Two-track, non-electrified rail line running 8.5 miles through Queens from Long Island City to Jamaica

tool_args.label: Lower Montauk Branch Passenger Rail Reactivation
tool_args.raw_text: This study, conducted by the engineering firm AECOM on behalf of New York City Department of Transportation (NYCDOT) and sponsored by New York City District 30 Council Member Elizabeth Crowley, examines the feasibility of returning passenger service to the Lower Montauk Branch rail corridor

payload.project_name: Lower Montauk Branch Passenger Rail Reactivation
payload.description: Study of feasibility of reactivating passenger rail service on the Lower Montauk Branch in Queens, NY
payload.lead_agency: New York City Department of Transportation

warnings: project.lead_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Lead agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: Projected Weekday Ridership 2025
tool_args.raw_text: Estimated ridership for the Branch is 21,000 weekday and 13,000 weekend daily riders, based on a modeled build year of 2025

payload.description: Estimated weekday ridership for the Branch in modeled build year 2025

tool_args.label: Projected Weekend Daily Ridership 2025
tool_args.raw_text: Estimated ridership for the Branch is 21,000 weekday and 13,000 weekend daily riders

payload.description: Estimated weekend daily ridership for the Branch

tool_args.label: Projected Annual Ridership
tool_args.raw_text: Annual 5,820,000

payload.description: Projected annual ridership for the Branch

tool_args.label: Projected Annual Fare Revenue
tool_args.raw_text: Annual fare revenues are estimated at approximately $15 million (in 2017 dollars). This is an initial high-end estimate that assumes a subway-level fare ($2.75).

payload.description: Annual fare revenues estimated at approximately $15 million (in 2017 dollars) assuming subway-level fare ($2.75)

tool_args.label: Total Capital Costs - Passenger Only
tool_args.raw_text: In total, the capital costs for these upgrades would total $1.1B (2017 dollars).

payload.description: Total capital costs for passenger rail service including transit infrastructure, fleet, maintenance equipment, and yard/maintenance facility

tool_args.label: Total Capital Costs - Joint Passenger-Freight
tool_args.raw_text: Capital costs while maintaining freight service on the Branch are estimated at $2.2 billion

payload.description: Capital costs while maintaining freight service on the Branch, including upgrades to rail infrastructure, new running track, new freight yard, transit vehicles, storage and maintenance yard, and property acquisition

tool_args.label: Annual O&M Costs - 1-Car Trains

payload.raw_value_text: $48.0 million (annual, for one-car trains average of peers)
payload.description: Projected annual O&M costs for one-car trains (average of peers)

tool_args.label: Annual O&M Costs - Variable-Consist Trains

payload.raw_value_text: $54.6 million (annual, for variable-consist trains average of peers)
payload.description: Projected annual O&M costs for variable-consist trains (average of peers)

tool_args.label: Value Capture Bond Support - Total from 5 Key Station Areas
tool_args.raw_text: Initial analysis of total assessed real estate value surrounding five key stations... could potentially support approximately $309 million in bonds

payload.scope: 5 Key Station Areas (Grand Ave/Flushing Ave, Fresh Pond/Metro Ave, Metro Mall, 80th Street, Woodhaven Blvd)
payload.description: Preliminary estimate of projected bond support from value capture financing at five key station areas

tool_args.label: Value Capture - Woodhaven Blvd Station Area
tool_args.raw_text: Woodhaven Blvd. $100,000,000

payload.description: Projected bond support from Woodhaven Blvd station area

tool_args.label: Value Capture - 80th Street Station Area

payload.description: Projected bond support from 80th Street station area

tool_args.label: Recommended Rolling Stock: FRA-compliant Diesel Multiple Unit (DMU)
tool_args.raw_text: this study recommended FRA-compliant, diesel-powered Diesel Multiple Unit (DMU) equipment, which is currently in use in numerous cities around the world.

payload.claim_text: This study recommended FRA-compliant, diesel-powered Diesel Multiple Unit (DMU) equipment
payload.subject: Lower Montauk Branch passenger service
payload.description: Recommended rolling stock for passenger service on the Lower Montauk Branch

tool_args.label: Proposed Service Plan: 5am-10pm, 6-min peak/15-min off-peak headways
tool_args.raw_text: A passenger service plan from 5:00am to 10:00pm, providing 6-minute peak and 15-minute off-peak headways, would capture the bulk of potential ridership while leaving open the overnight hours.

payload.claim_text: A passenger service plan from 5:00am to 10:00pm, providing 6-minute peak and 15-minute off-peak headways
payload.subject: Lower Montauk Branch passenger service concept
payload.description: Proposed passenger service span and headways

tool_args.label: Feasibility Finding: Joint Passenger-Freight Operations Feasible
tool_args.raw_text: Initial analysis shows that it would be feasible to develop joint passenger-freight operations on the Branch

payload.claim_text: Initial analysis shows that it would be feasible to develop joint passenger-freight operations on the Branch, allowing for robust transit service while maintaining and upgrading freight operations if desired.
payload.subject: Lower Montauk Branch
payload.description: Key feasibility finding of the study

tool_args.label: Proposed Stations: 10 Station Locations
tool_args.raw_text: recommended 10 station locations: the two termini stations at Long Island City and Jamaica and eight intermediate stations located an average of one mile apart

payload.claim_text: Recommended 10 station locations: the two termini stations at Long Island City and Jamaica and eight intermediate stations located an average of one mile apart
payload.subject: Lower Montauk Branch passenger service
payload.description: Recommended station locations for the proposed passenger service

tool_args.label: Additional Freight Infrastructure Costs
tool_args.raw_text: additional substantial investments would be necessary. This scenario would require additional running track in some areas and new freight rail yards, and would require an additional $1.0B in upgrades.

payload.description: Additional capital investments needed for freight service maintained outside passenger service hours

tool_args.label: Air Rights Sale Generated $56M for MTA in 2015
tool_args.raw_text: In 2015, the MTA sold several small parcels and air rights to the developer of an adjacent property. The sale allowed an additional 39 stories to the high rise building, while also generating an additional $56M for the Transit Authority.

payload.claim_text: In 2015, the MTA sold several small parcels and air rights to the developer of an adjacent property. The sale allowed an additional 39 stories to the high rise building, while also generating an additional $56M for the Transit Authority.
payload.subject: MTA air rights sale
payload.description: Example of development rights transfer used to raise revenue for MTA

tool_args.label: Study Limitations: Many Factors Outside Scope
tool_args.raw_text: Many factors in a reactivation were outside the scope of this study, or could not be evaluated in detail

payload.gap_text: Many factors in a reactivation were outside the scope of this study, or could not be evaluated in detail
payload.missing_information: Structural bridge analysis, zoning change studies, freight network demand changes, final station designs, value capture analysis, LIRR/Jamaica terminal access modifications, capital cost refinements, governance/operations plan, funding and permitting processes
payload.description: The study identifies multiple factors that need further analysis in future studies
